description: Persist per-board inbound reopen policy fields for enablement, cutoff duration, optional reopen status, and AI acknowledgement suppression.
Expose board settings UI and server actions for editing inbound reopen policy using board-owned ticket statuses.
Load matched ticket board policy during inbound reply handling before comment creation.
When reopen-on-reply is disabled for a board, attach threaded replies to closed tickets without reopening.
Apply the per-board cutoff window so replies older than the configured threshold bypass closed-ticket attachment and enter the new-ticket path.
Always reopen matched closed tickets for internal-user replies that fall within the cutoff window.
Reopen matched closed tickets for client/contact replies within the cutoff window when AI suppression is disabled or not applicable.
Resolve reopen target status from board `reopen_status_id` and fall back to the board default open status when unset.
Apply reopen transitions using existing ticket status semantics so `status_id`, `is_closed`, `closed_at`, and `closed_by` stay aligned.
Preserve existing inbound dedupe and token-only self-notification skipping before reopen decisions execute.
Introduce a shared acknowledgement-decision interface with a default non-AI implementation that never suppresses reopen.
Provide an EE acknowledgement-decision implementation behind manual edition-aware resolution so AI logic stays isolated in `@ee/...` code.
Require both board AI suppression enablement and tenant `AI Assistant` entitlement before any acknowledgement LLM call.
Use the standard LLM path with a minimal prompt and tiny formatted output such as `ACK` or `NOT_ACK` for short acknowledgement-like client replies.
Treat `ACK` client replies as comment-only updates that keep the ticket closed.
Treat invalid AI output, missing EE code, missing entitlement, or AI runtime errors as reopen-normal fallback behavior.
Record decision metadata sufficient to explain reopen, cutoff, and AI suppression outcomes during debugging.
